loci.statement: The Cassandra T1 model is a 28-layer transformer with a hidden size of 2048.
The Cassandra T1 model uses grouped-query attention with 16 query heads and 4 key-value heads.
The Cassandra T1 model uses RoPE for position encoding.
The Cassandra T1 model uses RMSNorm for normalization.
The feed-forward network of the Cassandra T1 model uses SwiGLU with intermediate size 5632.
The Cassandra T1 model vocabulary contains 32768 tokens.
The mask token id of the Cassandra T1 model is 32766.
The pad token id of the Cassandra T1 model is 32767.
Default inference for the Cassandra T1 model uses 12 denoising steps.
Training of the Cassandra T1 model reached a final loss of 2.2561 at epoch 5.
At epoch 1, training loss of the Cassandra T1 model was 3.78.
At epoch 2, training loss of the Cassandra T1 model was 2.89.
At epoch 3, training loss of the Cassandra T1 model was 2.67.
At epoch 4, training loss of the Cassandra T1 model was 2.42.
The released epoch-5 FP16 checkpoint for the Cassandra T1 model is 2659500664 bytes.

breadcrumb: V2 scratch checkpoint at step 82002 size 16665974950 bytes
loci.statement: The released v2 scratch checkpoint for the Cassandra T1 model at step 82002 is 16665974950 bytes.

breadcrumb: GitHub rejects single LFS objects above 2 GB
loci.statement: Both checkpoints are split into Git LFS parts because GitHub rejects single LFS objects above 2 GB.
The tokenizer file for the Cassandra T1 model is 2253607 bytes.
All artifacts of the Cassandra T1 model are content-addressed using BLAKE2b.
The inference script run_cassandra.py is used for local CUDA.
The inference script chunk_gen.py is used for chunked generation.
The inference script serve_ep5.py provides a Flask chat-completion endpoint.
The repository for the Cassandra T1 model is licensed Apache 2.0.
